project.name: ECC Hook System
project.description: Event-driven hook subsystem of ECC (Everything Claude Code): ~48 Node.js scripts (plus one Python security monitor and one shell wrapper) implementing Claude Code lifecycle hooks — consolidated pre/post-bash dispatchers, blocking gates (config protection, gateguard fact-force, no-verify block), observation/telemetry hooks, session lifecycle handlers, and quality gates.

nodes.name: auto-tmux-dev.js
nodes.summary: PreToolUse Bash hook that detects long-running dev-server commands (npm/pnpm/yarn dev, etc.) and transparently re-runs them inside a detached tmux session so the agent's foreground stays unblocked. Observes and rewrites rather than blocking.
nodes.languageNotes: CommonJS hook exporting run(rawInput); uses spawnSync to launch the wrapped command in a tmux session.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Entry point invoked by the dispatcher: parses the Bash tool input, matches dev-server commands, and spawns them in a named tmux session, returning the rewritten/observed hook result.

nodes.name: bash-hook-dispatcher.js
nodes.summary: Central fan-out dispatcher for all Bash lifecycle hooks: it requires every sibling pre/post-Bash check, runs them in registered order honoring per-hook profile gating and ECC_DISABLED_HOOKS, and merges their results (block decisions, stderr, additionalContext) into a single PreToolUse/PostToolUse response.
nodes.languageNotes: Aggregates 10 sibling hook modules; normalizeHookResult unifies exit codes and block decisions across heterogeneous check outputs.

nodes.name: normalizeHookResult
nodes.summary: Normalizes a single sibling hook's return value into a canonical {output, stderr, exitCode} shape, coercing Buffers/strings and validating block decisions and exit codes.

nodes.name: runHooks
nodes.summary: Iterates a hook registry, skips profile-disabled hooks via isHookEnabled, invokes each hook's run(), short-circuits on a block decision, and accumulates additionalContext for the combined PreToolUse output.

nodes.name: runPreBash
nodes.summary: Public entry consumed by pre-bash-dispatcher.js; runs the PRE_BASH_HOOKS registry (block-no-verify, auto-tmux-dev, reminders, commit-quality, gateguard) over the raw stdin.

nodes.name: runPostBash
nodes.summary: Public entry consumed by post-bash-dispatcher.js; runs the POST_BASH_HOOKS registry (command-log audit/cost, pr-created, build-complete) over the raw stdin.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Standalone CLI path: reads stdin, dispatches to runPreBash or runPostBash based on invocation mode, writes merged stderr/stdout and sets the process exit code.

nodes.name: block-no-verify.js
nodes.summary: PreToolUse Bash gate that parses git commit/push commands and BLOCKS (exit 2 / deny) any attempt to bypass hooks via --no-verify, -n, or a --git-hooks-path override, using a custom shell tokenizer to resist obfuscation.
nodes.languageNotes: Hand-rolled shell tokenizer (tokenizeShellWords, findGit, isInComment) defends against quoting/comment tricks used to smuggle --no-verify past the gate.

nodes.name: tokenizeShellWords
nodes.summary: Custom shell-word tokenizer honoring single/double quotes and escapes, used to split git command segments so flag detection cannot be evaded by quoting.

nodes.name: detectGitCommand
nodes.summary: Locates a genuine git invocation within an arbitrary shell command, skipping comments and validating the preceding context so only real git commit/push commands are evaluated.

nodes.name: hasNoVerifyFlag
nodes.summary: Scans a detected git command's tokens for --no-verify or its short-flag equivalents, accounting for value-consuming options and inline-value forms to avoid false negatives.

nodes.name: hasHooksPathOverride
nodes.summary: Detects attempts to neutralize git hooks via core.hooksPath overrides (-c core.hooksPath=... or --git-hooks-path), another bypass route this gate blocks.

nodes.name: checkCommand
nodes.summary: Top-level decision function: detects the git command and returns a block result if a no-verify flag or hooks-path override is present, otherwise allows.

nodes.name: extractCommand
nodes.summary: Parses the hook's raw stdin JSON to extract the Bash tool's command string for inspection.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Exported hook entry invoked by the dispatcher: extracts the command and returns checkCommand's allow/deny result.

nodes.name: gateguard-fact-force.js
nodes.summary: PreToolUse fact-forcing gate covering Bash, Edit, and Write: on the first destructive/sensitive action of a session it BLOCKS (deny) and demands the agent present grounding facts (callers, rollback, verbatim instruction), tracking per-session state to allow the retry. Detects destructive rm/git/find-exec/SQL and protects .claude/settings paths.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest hook in the cluster; persists per-session denial state to disk with atomic writes, sha256 session/command keys, and budget-limited recovery via ECC_GATEGUARD=off.

nodes.name: isDestructiveGit
nodes.summary: Classifies a tokenized git command as destructive (force push, hard reset, clean -f, branch -D, etc.), distinguishing value-consuming flags from destructive ones.

nodes.name: isDestructiveBash
nodes.summary: Top-level destructiveness classifier for a Bash command: explodes subshells, strips quotes, and checks for destructive SQL DDL, rm, git, and find -exec patterns across all executable bodies.

nodes.name: isReadOnlyGitIntrospection
nodes.summary: Allowlists safe read-only git introspection (status/diff/log with vetted args) so the routine-Bash fact gate does not nag on harmless inspection commands.

nodes.name: loadState
nodes.summary: Loads the per-session gate state from disk, expiring stale state files by TTL so denial tracking resets between sessions.

nodes.name: saveState
nodes.summary: Atomically persists gate state (checked keys, denial count) via a temp-file-and-rename, merging concurrent updates and pruning old entries to bound file growth.

nodes.name: denyResult
nodes.summary: Builds the structured deny/block hook response carrying the fact-forcing message plus the ECC_GATEGUARD recovery hint.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Exported hook entry invoked by the dispatcher: parses input, honors disable flags, classifies the Bash/Edit/Write action, and either allows or returns a fact-forcing deny based on per-session checked state and denial budget.

nodes.name: post-bash-build-complete.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse Bash hook that observes completed build commands (npm/pnpm/yarn build) and emits a non-blocking notification; never affects the tool result.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Hook entry: parses the executed Bash command, and if it was a build command emits a completion notice as a passive PostToolUse observation.

nodes.name: post-bash-command-log.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse Bash hook that appends executed commands to an audit/cost log under ~/.claude, redacting secrets (tokens, AWS keys, GitHub PATs, passwords) before writing. Pure observation, never blocks.
nodes.languageNotes: sanitizeCommand applies a redaction regex chain so logged commands never persist credentials.

nodes.name: sanitizeCommand
nodes.summary: Redacts secrets from a command string (tokens, AWS access keys, GitHub PATs, passwords, Authorization headers) before it is written to the audit log.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Hook entry: parses the executed command, sanitizes it, and appends a timestamped line to the audit or cost log under the user's home directory.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Standalone CLI path: reads stdin and invokes run when the script is executed directly rather than via the dispatcher.

nodes.name: post-bash-dispatcher.js
nodes.summary: Thin PostToolUse Bash entry point: reads stdin and delegates to bash-hook-dispatcher's runPostBash, which fans out to the post-bash check scripts. Non-blocking.

nodes.name: post-bash-pr-created.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse Bash hook that observes `gh pr create` invocations, parses the resulting PR URL from output, and emits a non-blocking notice with a review command. Pure observation.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Hook entry: detects a `gh pr create` command, extracts the PR URL and number from the command output, and returns a passive notice suggesting how to review the PR.

nodes.name: pre-bash-commit-quality.js
nodes.summary: PreToolUse Bash hook (strict profile) that, on git commit, lints staged files and validates the commit message: it flags console.log/debugger/TODO and conventional-commit violations, and can BLOCK the commit when staged files fail linting.
nodes.languageNotes: Resolves linter executables across PATH/PATHEXT for cross-platform support; inspects staged content via git show :file.

nodes.name: findFileIssues
nodes.summary: Scans a staged file's content line by line for committed console.log, debugger statements, unresolved TODO/FIXME markers, and other configured anti-patterns.

nodes.name: validateCommitMessage
nodes.summary: Extracts the commit message from a git commit command and validates it against conventional-commit rules (type prefix, capitalization, trailing punctuation).

nodes.name: resolveCommand
nodes.summary: Cross-platform executable resolver that walks PATH and PATHEXT candidates to locate a linter binary before spawning it.

nodes.name: runLinter
nodes.summary: Runs the project's available linter (eslint and friends) against staged JS/TS files via spawnSync and returns the captured output for evaluation.

nodes.name: evaluate
nodes.summary: Core decision logic: on a git commit, gathers staged files, runs file-issue scans, message validation, and linting, then decides whether to warn or block the commit.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Exported hook entry invoked by the dispatcher: delegates to evaluate and returns its allow/block result.

nodes.name: pre-bash-dispatcher.js
nodes.summary: Thin PreToolUse Bash entry point: reads stdin and delegates to bash-hook-dispatcher's runPreBash, which fans out to the pre-bash check scripts (block-no-verify, gateguard, commit-quality, reminders). Can block via the aggregated result.

nodes.name: doc-file-warning.js
nodes.summary: PreToolUse (Write matcher) hook that warns when Claude attempts to write an ad-hoc documentation filename (NOTES/TODO/SCRATCH/etc.) outside structured directories. Observes only — always exits 0 and surfaces a non-blocking additionalContext warning.
nodes.languageNotes: Dual-mode CommonJS hook: exports run(rawInput) for in-process execution via run-with-flags.js and also wires a stdin/spawnSync fallback. Uses a denylist regex to avoid false positives in markdown-heavy workflows.

nodes.name: isSuspiciousDocPath
nodes.summary: Returns true only for known ad-hoc doc filenames (.md/.txt matching NOTES/TODO/SCRATCH/etc.) located outside structured directories like docs/, .claude/, skills/.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Exportable hook entrypoint that parses the PreToolUse payload, checks the target file_path, and returns a non-blocking additionalContext warning when an ad-hoc doc filename is detected; always exitCode 0.

nodes.name: pre-bash-git-push-reminder.js
nodes.summary: PreToolUse (Bash matcher) reminder hook that detects `git push` commands and injects a non-blocking review reminder into additionalContext. Never blocks — passes the command through and exits 0.
nodes.languageNotes: CommonJS hook with require.main guard: runs the stdin path only when invoked directly, while still exporting run() for the run-with-flags.js in-process wrapper.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Parses the Bash tool input, and if the command matches a `git push` pattern returns an additionalContext review reminder; otherwise passes the raw payload through unchanged. Always non-blocking.

nodes.name: pre-bash-tmux-reminder.js
nodes.summary: PreToolUse (Bash matcher) reminder hook that suggests running long-lived commands (npm/pnpm/yarn install/test, cargo build, docker, pytest, etc.) inside tmux when not already in a tmux session. Observes only — non-blocking, exit 0.
nodes.languageNotes: Gates on process.platform and process.env.TMUX so it is silent on Windows or inside an existing tmux session. CommonJS with require.main guard plus exported run().

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Parses the Bash tool input and, on non-Windows hosts outside a tmux session, returns a non-blocking tmux suggestion when the command matches long-running build/test patterns; otherwise passes through.

nodes.name: pre-write-doc-warn.js
nodes.summary: Backward-compatible thin entrypoint that simply requires doc-file-warning.js, preserving the legacy PreToolUse (Write) hook path for consumers still referencing this filename.

nodes.name: pretooluse-visible-output.js
nodes.summary: Shared helper used by the PreToolUse reminder/warning hooks to normalize and combine additionalContext strings and serialize them into the hookSpecificOutput JSON envelope Claude Code expects. Output-shaping utility, never blocking on its own.
nodes.languageNotes: CommonJS module exporting three pure functions; centralizes the PreToolUse additionalContext envelope so individual hooks need not hand-roll the JSON shape.

nodes.name: normalizeAdditionalContext
nodes.summary: Normalizes an additionalContext value (array or scalar) into a single trimmed, newline-joined string, dropping empty entries.

nodes.name: buildPreToolUseAdditionalContext
nodes.summary: Serializes normalized additionalContext into the PreToolUse hookSpecificOutput JSON envelope (hookEventName: PreToolUse) that Claude Code surfaces; returns an empty string when there is no context.

nodes.name: run-with-flags.js
nodes.summary: Generic hook runner/wrapper that executes a target hook script only when enabled by ECC hook-profile flags (ECC_HOOK_PROFILE / ECC_DISABLED_HOOKS), preferring fast in-process require()+run() and falling back to spawnSync. Honors a dry-run mode and propagates the wrapped hook's exit code, so it can be a gate or an observer depending on the hook it wraps.
nodes.languageNotes: Resolves CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT and confines target scripts to the plugin root (startsWith check) to prevent path escape. Uses an explicit stdout-write-then-exit callback to avoid truncating large pass-through payloads past the ~64KB pipe buffer.

nodes.name: readStdinRaw
nodes.summary: Reads the hook's stdin payload as a UTF-8 string with a 1MB cap, resolving a {raw, truncated} promise and never rejecting on stream error.

nodes.name: exitWithStdout
nodes.summary: Writes the full stdout payload and exits only after the chunk flushes, preventing truncation of large pass-through JSON past the pipe buffer.

nodes.name: resolveHookResult
nodes.summary: Interprets the object returned by an in-process hook run(): writes stderr, derives the exit code, and builds the PreToolUse additionalContext envelope or stdout pass-through accordingly.

nodes.name: extractTargetContext
nodes.summary: Parses the incoming hook payload to extract human-readable target context (tool name, command, or file path) used for dry-run preview messages.

nodes.name: buildDryRunPreview
nodes.summary: Assembles a human-readable preview string describing which hook would run against which target and profiles, used when the wrapper is invoked in dry-run mode.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the wrapper: reads stdin, checks profile/disabled gating and dry-run, resolves and path-confines the target script, runs it in-process via run() when available or falls back to spawnSync, then emits the resolved output and exit code.

nodes.name: check-console-log.js
nodes.summary: Stop-lifecycle hook that scans git-modified JS/TS files for leftover console.log statements and emits non-blocking warnings; excludes tests, configs, and scripts/. Observes only (exit 0, pass-through).
nodes.languageNotes: Reads raw stdin with a 1MB cap and always passes the original payload through on exit.

nodes.name: passThroughAndExit
nodes.summary: Re-emits the captured stdin payload and exits 0, the pass-through path used after warnings are printed to stderr.

nodes.name: check-hook-enabled.js
nodes.summary: Tiny CLI helper that prints 'yes'/'no' depending on whether a given hook id is enabled under the active ECC hook profiles, used to gate hook execution from settings.

nodes.name: config-protection.js
nodes.summary: PreToolUse Edit/Write gate that BLOCKS (exit 2) modifications to existing linter/formatter config files (ESLint, Prettier, Biome, Ruff, etc.), steering the agent to fix source instead of relaxing checks. Allows first-time creation.

nodes.name: parseInput
nodes.summary: Parses the hook's stdin payload, accepting either a raw JSON string or a pre-parsed object.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Core gate logic: resolves the edited file basename against the protected-config set and emits a block decision (exit 2) when an existing protected config is being modified.

nodes.name: cost-tracker.js
nodes.summary: Stop-lifecycle telemetry hook that sums token usage across the session transcript JSONL and appends a cumulative cost row to ~/.claude/metrics/costs.jsonl, preferring an authoritative harness-cost cache when fresh. Observes only.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents a real-world bug fix: switched from absent Stop-payload usage fields to summing the transcript file Claude Code passes.

nodes.name: readHarnessCost
nodes.summary: Reads the per-session harness-cost cache file from the temp dir and returns the authoritative cost only when the cache is fresh within the max-age window.

nodes.name: sumUsageFromTranscript
nodes.summary: Parses the session transcript JSONL line by line and accumulates input/output and cache token counts across all assistant turns.

nodes.name: cursor-session-env.js
nodes.summary: Cursor SessionStart hook that resolves and injects ECC_AGENT_DATA_HOME into the composer session env so memory, learned skills, and metrics persist under a dedicated data root. Observes only.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Reads stdin, builds the Cursor session-env payload with the resolved agent data home, and writes it to stdout with explanatory additional_context.

nodes.name: design-quality-check.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse Edit/Write hook that scans edited frontend files (astro/css/html/jsx/scss/svelte/tsx/vue) for generic-template drift heuristics and emits a non-blocking design-quality reminder. Self-contained, no network. Observes only.

nodes.name: getFilePaths
nodes.summary: Extracts edited file paths from the tool input, handling both single file_path and MultiEdit edits arrays.

nodes.name: buildWarning
nodes.summary: Composes the multi-line design-quality reminder message listing the touched frontend files, the design checklist, and detected generic signals.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Core hook logic: filters edited paths to frontend extensions, reads their content, detects generic-template signals, and emits the warning when frontend files were modified.

nodes.name: desktop-notify.js
nodes.summary: Stop-lifecycle hook that sends a native desktop notification with the task summary when Claude finishes: macOS via iTerm2 escape sequence or osascript, WSL via PowerShell BurntToast. Observes only.
nodes.languageNotes: Cross-platform notification with multiplexer (tmux/screen) detection and per-OS delivery fallbacks.

nodes.name: findPowerShell
nodes.summary: Locates an available PowerShell binary (pwsh or Windows PowerShell) under WSL for delivering toast notifications.

nodes.name: notifyWindows
nodes.summary: Sends a Windows toast notification via PowerShell BurntToast, escaping the title and body for the PowerShell command.

nodes.name: extractSummary
nodes.summary: Derives a short notification summary line from the assistant message, taking the first meaningful line and truncating it.

nodes.name: findTerminalTTY
nodes.summary: Walks up the process tree via ps to locate the controlling terminal TTY, used to route iTerm2 notifications to the right tab.

nodes.name: notifyMacOS
nodes.summary: Delivers a macOS notification, preferring the iTerm2 native escape sequence written to the terminal TTY and falling back to osascript.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Core hook logic: parses stdin, extracts the summary, and dispatches the platform-appropriate notification (macOS or WSL/Windows).

nodes.name: ecc-context-monitor.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse context-monitor hook that reads the metrics bridge file and injects agent-facing warnings when thresholds cross: context exhaustion, high cost, scope creep, or tool loops. Observes/warns only.

nodes.name: writeWarnState
nodes.summary: Atomically writes the per-session warn-state file via a temp file and rename-with-retry to avoid partial writes.

nodes.name: detectLoop
nodes.summary: Inspects the recent tool-call signature counts to detect repeated identical tool invocations indicating a stuck loop.

nodes.name: evaluateConditions
nodes.summary: Evaluates the bridge metrics against context/cost/scope/loop thresholds and returns a severity-sorted list of warnings.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Core hook logic: reads the bridge, evaluates threshold conditions, de-duplicates against prior warn-state, and injects the top warnings into the agent context.

nodes.name: ecc-metrics-bridge.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse telemetry hook that maintains a running per-session metrics aggregate in a temp bridge file (cost, files touched, recent tool hashes), avoiding repeated JSONL scans. Read by the statusline and context monitor. Observes only.

nodes.name: stableStringify
nodes.summary: Produces a deterministic, key-sorted string representation of a value for stable hashing of tool inputs.

nodes.name: hashToolCall
nodes.summary: Computes a stable SHA-256 signature for a tool invocation (special-casing edit/write payloads) used for loop detection and dedupe.

nodes.name: extractFilePaths
nodes.summary: Pulls touched file paths from a Write/Edit/MultiEdit tool input for tracking files modified during the session.

nodes.name: writeCostWarningIfChanged
nodes.summary: Emits a cost warning to stderr only when its signature differs from the cached one, preventing duplicate per-turn warnings.

nodes.name: readSessionCost
nodes.summary: Reads and sums session cost/token usage from the costs JSONL, warning when the file is missing or contains malformed lines.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Core hook logic: updates the per-session bridge aggregate with touched files, recent tool hashes, and refreshed cost, writing it atomically.

nodes.name: ecc-statusline.js
nodes.summary: statusLine command (not a hook event) that renders model, current task, cost/token counts, directory, and a context-usage bar by reading the metrics bridge file plus Claude Code runtime stdin. Observes only.
nodes.languageNotes: Registered under settings.json statusLine rather than hooks.json; uses Unicode block characters to draw the context bar.

nodes.name: formatDuration
nodes.summary: Formats the elapsed time since an ISO timestamp into a compact human string like '5s', '12m', or '1h23m'.

nodes.name: buildContextBar
nodes.summary: Builds the Unicode progress bar and percentage string representing remaining context window.

nodes.name: readCurrentTask
nodes.summary: Finds the most recent agent todo file for the session and returns the active in-progress task label for display.

nodes.name: runStatusline
nodes.summary: Core statusline logic: reads runtime stdin and the metrics bridge, assembles the model/task/cost/dir/context segments, and writes the rendered line to stdout.

nodes.name: evaluate-session.js
nodes.summary: Stop-lifecycle continuous-learning hook that reads the session transcript and extracts reusable patterns into the learned-skills directory. Chosen on Stop (once per session) over UserPromptSubmit for lower latency. Observes only.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that parses the Stop payload, resolves the learned-skills config and directory, and counts/extracts patterns from the session before exiting 0.

nodes.name: governance-capture.js
nodes.summary: PreToolUse/PostToolUse security hook that detects governance-relevant events (hardcoded secrets, policy violations, security findings, approval-required ops) and writes them to the governance events store. Gated by ECC_GOVERNANCE_CAPTURE. Observes/records only.

nodes.name: detectSecrets
nodes.summary: Scans text against secret patterns (AWS keys, generic secrets, private keys, JWTs) and returns the matched finding names.

nodes.name: detectApprovalRequired
nodes.summary: Matches a command against approval-required patterns and returns the findings that should trigger an approval-requested event.

nodes.name: summarizeCommand
nodes.summary: Produces a truncated, fingerprinted summary of a command for safe logging in governance events without leaking full payloads.

nodes.name: analyzeForGovernanceEvents
nodes.summary: Inspects a tool invocation for secrets, sensitive paths, approval-required operations, and security-relevant commands, building the list of governance events to emit.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Core hook logic: gated by ECC_GOVERNANCE_CAPTURE, parses input, runs analysis on PreToolUse, and emits the resulting governance events.

nodes.name: insaits-security-monitor.py
nodes.summary: PreToolUse (Bash|Write|Edit|MultiEdit) security gate that runs local InsAIts anomaly detection on tool input (credential exposure, prompt injection, hallucination chains, 20+ types). BLOCKS with exit 2 on critical findings; warns via stderr otherwise. Writes a forensic audit JSONL.
nodes.languageNotes: Python hook with configurable fail-open/fail-closed behavior via INSAITS_FAIL_MODE; exposes module-level functions for testing.

nodes.name: extract_content
nodes.summary: Extracts the inspectable text content from a tool-input payload across Bash command, Write/Edit content, and MultiEdit edits shapes.

nodes.name: write_audit
nodes.summary: Appends a hash-enriched, timestamped audit event to the forensic session JSONL, swallowing IO errors with a warning.

nodes.name: get_anomaly_attr
nodes.summary: Reads an attribute from an anomaly object or dict uniformly, tolerating both shapes returned by the detector.

nodes.name: format_feedback
nodes.summary: Formats detected anomalies into a human-readable stderr feedback block shown to the agent.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point: reads stdin JSON, extracts content, runs the InsAIts monitor, writes the audit event, and exits 2 to block on critical anomalies or 0 with warnings otherwise (respecting fail-mode).

nodes.name: insaits-security-wrapper.js
nodes.summary: Thin Node bridge that receives hook stdin and delegates to insaits-security-monitor.py, existing because run-with-flags.js spawns hooks via node and needs a JS entry point. Forwards exit codes (including block exit 2).

nodes.name: mcp-health-check.js
nodes.summary: PreToolUse / PostToolUseFailure hook that probes MCP server health before MCP tool calls and, on failure, marks servers unhealthy and attempts reconnect+re-probe. Persists health state across compaction; fails open or blocks based on configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest file in the batch; spawns command MCP servers safely (rejects unsafe shell chars) and probes HTTP servers treating auth-gated codes as reachable.

nodes.name: loadState
nodes.summary: Loads the persisted MCP health-state map from disk, returning a normalized empty structure on missing or invalid data.

nodes.name: readRawStdin
nodes.summary: Reads the raw hook stdin payload with a size cap, supporting an env override for synchronous test injection.

nodes.name: extractMcpTarget
nodes.summary: Derives the target MCP server name from the tool name (mcp__<server>__<tool>) and input fields.

nodes.name: markUnhealthy
nodes.summary: Records a server as unhealthy with an exponential backoff next-retry timestamp and the failure code/message.

nodes.name: requestHttp
nodes.summary: Issues a timeout-bounded HTTP/HTTPS reachability probe and resolves with whether the status code indicates a healthy or reachable server.

nodes.name: probeCommandServer
nodes.summary: Spawns a stdio command MCP server with retries and timeout, rejecting unsafe shell characters, to verify it can start successfully.

nodes.name: probeServer
nodes.summary: Dispatches to the HTTP or command probe based on the resolved server config and classifies the result as healthy or reconnectable.

nodes.name: attemptReconnect
nodes.summary: Runs the per-server reconnect command (synchronously, with timeout) and reports success or the trimmed failure output.

nodes.name: handlePreToolUse
nodes.summary: PreToolUse path: respects backoff, probes the target server, marks health, attempts reconnect when down, and decides fail-open vs block.

nodes.name: handlePostToolUseFailure
nodes.summary: PostToolUseFailure path: classifies the failure, marks the server unhealthy, attempts reconnect and re-probe, and updates persisted state.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point: reads stdin, extracts the MCP target, and routes to the PreToolUse or PostToolUseFailure handler, emitting logs and the appropriate exit decision.

nodes.name: observe-runner.js
nodes.summary: Hook adapter that resolves the plugin root and spawns the continuous-learning-v2 observe.sh shell hook for the current lifecycle phase, relaying its stdout/stderr/exit. Observes only (non-blocking telemetry).

nodes.name: getPluginRoot
nodes.summary: Resolves the plugin root directory from explicit options or the CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT / ECC_PLUGIN_ROOT environment variables.

nodes.name: findShellBinary
nodes.summary: Locates a usable bash binary across env overrides and common paths to run the observe shell hook cross-platform.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Core logic: resolves the observe.sh target within the plugin root, spawns it with the phase and stdin, and captures its result for relay.

nodes.name: emitHookResult
nodes.summary: Relays the child hook's stderr/stdout to the parent streams and exits with its status, defaulting to a safe pass-through.

nodes.name: plugin-hook-bootstrap.js
nodes.summary: Hook bootstrap that normalizes the plugin root, ensures the agent-data-home env, and spawns the real target hook (Node or shell) sandboxed within the plugin root, passing stdin through. Pass-through on failure.
nodes.languageNotes: Path-traversal guard via resolveTarget ensures spawned scripts stay inside the plugin root; platform-normalizes Windows drive letters.

nodes.name: passthrough
nodes.summary: Emits the child result's stdout and exits with its status, the default safe path when no specialized handling applies.

nodes.name: normalizePluginRootForPlatform
nodes.summary: Normalizes the plugin root path for the current platform, uppercasing Windows drive letters for consistent comparison.

nodes.name: resolveTarget
nodes.summary: Resolves a relative hook path against the plugin root and enforces that the result stays inside it, guarding against path traversal.

nodes.name: findShellBinary
nodes.summary: Locates a usable bash binary across env overrides and common locations for spawning shell-based hooks.

nodes.name: spawnNode
nodes.summary: Spawns the resolved Node hook script with the agent-data-home env injected and the raw stdin forwarded.

nodes.name: spawnShell
nodes.summary: Spawns the resolved shell hook via the detected bash binary with the agent-data-home env and forwarded stdin.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point: reads stdin, resolves the target hook from args, dispatches to spawnNode or spawnShell, and falls back to pass-through on errors.

nodes.name: post-edit-accumulator.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse Edit/Write hook that appends each edited JS/TS path to a session-scoped temp file so stop-format-typecheck.js can batch format+typecheck once at Stop, eliminating per-edit latency. Observes only.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses appendFileSync for atomic concurrent writes; dedup is deferred to the consuming Stop hook.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Parses the edit payload and appends each touched JS/TS file path (single or MultiEdit) to the session accumulation file.

nodes.name: post-edit-console-warn.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse Edit hook that warns (with line numbers) when an edited JS/TS file contains console.log statements, helping remove debug code before commit. Observes only (pass-through).

nodes.name: post-edit-format.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse Edit hook that auto-detects the project formatter (Biome or Prettier) and formats the edited JS/TS file, preferring the local .bin binary; for Biome uses check --write to fold in linting. Fails silently. Observes only.
nodes.languageNotes: Guards against cmd.exe command injection by rejecting unsafe path characters when spawning the formatter.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Core logic: resolves the project root and formatter for an edited JS/TS file and runs the formatter binary against it, passing input through.

nodes.name: post-edit-typecheck.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse Edit hook that, after editing a .ts/.tsx file, walks up to the nearest tsconfig.json and runs tsc --noEmit, reporting only errors that reference the edited file. Observes only (pass-through).
nodes.languageNotes: Avoids bare-basename matching to prevent false positives across same-named files; uses npx.cmd on Windows to dodge shell:true injection.

nodes.name: pre-bash-dev-server-block.js
nodes.summary: PreToolUse Bash gate that parses the command (including substitutions and subshells) and BLOCKS long-running dev-server launches (npm/pnpm/yarn/bun/npx dev/start, tmux launchers) to keep the agent from hanging on foreground servers.
nodes.languageNotes: Hand-rolled shell tokenizer handles quoting, env-var prefixes, and skippable command wrappers (env/sudo/exec) before classifying the leading command word.

nodes.name: readToken
nodes.summary: Reads a single shell token from an input string starting at an index, respecting single/double quoting and whitespace.

nodes.name: getLeadingCommandWord
nodes.summary: Determines the effective leading command word of a shell segment, skipping env-var assignments and wrapper prefixes like env/sudo/exec.

nodes.name: collectCheckSegments
nodes.summary: Expands a command into all shell segments to inspect, recursing into command substitutions and subshell groups.

nodes.name: isBlockedDevSegment
nodes.summary: Tests whether a shell segment launches a blocked dev server or tmux launcher based on its leading command word and patterns.

nodes.name: pre-compact.js
nodes.summary: PreCompact lifecycle hook that preserves session state (writes a marker and recent files index to the sessions dir) before Claude compacts context, so important state survives summarization. Observes only.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point: ensures the sessions directory exists and appends a pre-compaction marker plus recent-files context before exiting 0.

nodes.name: quality-gate.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse Edit hook running lightweight per-file quality checks (Biome/Prettier for .json/.md, plus Prettier/Go/Python checks); skips JS/TS+Biome since post-edit-format.js already covers it. Optionally strict/fix via env. Observes/warns.

nodes.name: maybeRunQualityGate
nodes.summary: Core logic: resolves the project root/formatter for the edited file and runs the appropriate per-language quality checks, honoring fix/strict env flags.

nodes.name: run-with-flags-shell.sh
nodes.summary: Bash entry shim for ECC hooks: reads stdin, asks check-hook-enabled.js whether the hook is active for the current profile, and dispatches to the target hook script with the lifecycle phase prefix. Passes stdin through unchanged (exit 0) when disabled or missing.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `set -euo pipefail` and resolves CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT with a fallback; fail-open design echoes stdin and exits 0 on any gating decision so it never blocks tool execution.

nodes.name: session-activity-tracker.js
nodes.summary: PostToolUse observe hook that records a sanitized per-tool activity row (redacted params, file events, git working-tree diff/patch previews) to ~/.claude/metrics/tool-usage.jsonl for ECC metric sync. Exports run() for the in-process run-with-flags wrapper.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy secret-redaction and diff/patch-preview pipeline; enriches file events by shelling out to git (spawnSync) to reconstruct working-tree patches before logging.

nodes.name: session-end-marker.js
nodes.summary: Lightweight SessionEnd hook that removes the current session's observer lease and stops the per-project observer once the final lease ends, then passes stdin through unchanged. Exports run() for in-process execution.
nodes.languageNotes: Delegates all lease/observer bookkeeping to ../lib/observer-sessions; wraps cleanup in try/catch so a failure is logged but never blocks (always returns the original stdin).

nodes.name: session-end.js
nodes.summary: Stop-event session-persistence hook that extracts a meaningful summary from the JSONL transcript (user messages, tools used, files modified) and writes/merges a per-session markdown file with ECC summary markers for cross-session continuity.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds and merges a markdown session header with ECC:SUMMARY:START/END comment markers using escaped-regexp replacement so repeated Stop calls update rather than duplicate the summary block.

nodes.name: session-start-bootstrap.js
nodes.summary: Bootstrap loader for the SessionStart hook: resolves the ECC plugin root (env var or well-known fallback paths) and delegates to scripts/hooks/run-with-flags.js with the session:start event, which gates and then runs session-start.js. Falls open by passing stdin through if the plugin root cannot be found.
nodes.languageNotes: Extracted from an inline `node -e` string in hooks.json specifically so the shell never sees `!` (history expansion) characters; scans the plugin cache directory tree to locate the runner root.

nodes.name: session-start.js
nodes.summary: SessionStart context-injection hook that loads bounded prior context into the new session: selects the most recent matching session summary, surfaces active instincts and learned skills, prunes expired session files, writes a session lease, and emits a character-limited additionalContext payload to stdout.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps injected prior-session summaries in an explicit HISTORICAL-REFERENCE-ONLY guard so stale task descriptions and ARGUMENTS= payloads are not re-executed; bounds total injected context via getSessionStartMaxContextChars.

nodes.name: stop-format-typecheck.js
nodes.summary: Stop-event quality hook that reads the post-edit accumulator and batch-formats edited JS/TS files (grouped by project root) and type-checks .ts/.tsx files (grouped by tsconfig dir, tsc --noEmit), reporting type errors to stderr. Exports run() so the runner uses require() and the 300s timeout governs the full batch.
nodes.languageNotes: Allocates per-batch timeouts proportional to batch count to stay under the Stop budget; guards against cmd.exe path-operator characters and clears the accumulator on read so repeated Stop calls do not double-process.

nodes.name: suggest-compact.js
nodes.summary: PreToolUse advisory hook that suggests manual compaction at logical intervals using two signals: a tool-call counter (first at a threshold, then periodically) and the primary context-size signal from the latest transcript usage record compared to a window-scaled token threshold. Emits a reminder via stdout, never blocks.
nodes.languageNotes: Persists per-session counters and context buckets to the temp dir with TTL-based cleanup; uses transcript usage tokens as the primary window-pressure signal since tool-call count is a weak proxy.

nodes.name: redactSecrets
nodes.summary: Redacts tokens, Authorization headers, AWS access-key IDs, passwords, and GitHub PAT prefixes from a string before it is logged.

nodes.name: sanitizeParamValue
nodes.summary: Recursively sanitizes a tool-input parameter value (arrays, objects, scalars), truncating and redacting each leaf before it is recorded in the activity log.

nodes.name: pushFileEvent
nodes.summary: Appends a deduplicated file-event record (path, action, optional diff and patch previews) to the events list, skipping URL-like and duplicate entries.

nodes.name: buildPatchPreviewFromReplacement
nodes.summary: Builds a sanitized unified-diff-style patch preview from old/new replacement strings, prefixing removed and added lines.

nodes.name: buildDiffPreviewFromPatchPreview
nodes.summary: Derives a compact human-readable diff preview (first removed and added line) from an already-built patch preview.

nodes.name: inferDefaultFileAction
nodes.summary: Maps a tool name (write, edit, multiedit, notebookedit, etc.) to a default file action label for activity records.

nodes.name: collectFilePaths
nodes.summary: Recursively walks a tool-input value collecting candidate file paths from known path-bearing keys, arrays, and nested objects.

nodes.name: fileEventPatchPreview
nodes.summary: Produces a patch preview for a file event based on tool action, handling replacement edits and file-creation content.

nodes.name: candidateGitPaths
nodes.summary: Computes the set of candidate path spellings (absolute, repo-relative, POSIX-normalized) for a file so git diff/ls-files lookups succeed across platforms.

nodes.name: patchPreviewFromGitDiff
nodes.summary: Runs git diff for the candidate paths and extracts hunk headers and added/removed lines into a bounded patch preview.

nodes.name: enrichFileEventFromWorkingTree
nodes.summary: Enriches a file event with diff and patch previews reconstructed from the git working tree when the tool input did not supply them.

nodes.name: collectFileEvents
nodes.summary: Recursively traverses tool input to build file-event records, attaching the inferred action plus diff/patch previews for each path-bearing key.

nodes.name: summarizeInput
nodes.summary: Builds a short human-readable summary line of a tool invocation from its name, input fields, and extracted file paths.

nodes.name: buildActivityRow
nodes.summary: Assembles the full sanitized activity record (event, tool, session id, file events, params, input/output summaries, timestamp) written to the metrics JSONL.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: In-process entry point: parses the PostToolUse stdin payload, builds the activity row, and appends it to the tool-usage metrics file. Returns input unchanged.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Legacy direct-CLI entry: reads bounded stdin and invokes run(), writing pass-through output to stdout.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: SessionEnd in-process entry: resolves the session id, removes its observer lease, and stops the per-project observer when no leases remain; returns stdin unchanged and never throws.

nodes.name: extractSessionSummary
nodes.summary: Parses the JSONL transcript to extract a session summary: recent user messages, tools used, and files modified, with ANSI stripping and bounded slicing.

nodes.name: getSessionMetadata
nodes.summary: Collects session metadata (project name, git branch, worktree) used to build the session-file header.

nodes.name: buildSessionHeader
nodes.summary: Builds the markdown session-file header (date, started, last-updated, project, branch, worktree), preserving an existing start time when present.

nodes.name: mergeSessionHeader
nodes.summary: Replaces the header region of existing session-file content with a freshly built header, or prepends one when no header exists.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the Stop-event session persistence: resolves the session file path from the transcript, extracts the summary, merges header and summary markers into the file, and writes it back.

nodes.name: buildSummarySection
nodes.summary: Renders the markdown summary section body (tasks, tools used, files modified) from an extracted summary object.

nodes.name: resolvePluginRoot
nodes.summary: Resolves the ECC plugin root directory by checking CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT, well-known plugin paths, and by scanning the plugin cache org/version directory tree for one containing run-with-flags.js.

nodes.name: dedupeRecentSessions
nodes.summary: Finds recent session files across the search dirs and deduplicates them by basename, keeping the most recently modified copy of each.

nodes.name: getSessionStartMode
nodes.summary: Parses the SessionStart stdin payload to determine the start mode (startup, resume, clear, compact) and detect skip/invalid cases.

nodes.name: limitSessionStartContext
nodes.summary: Truncates the assembled additionalContext payload to a configured maximum character budget, appending a truncation marker.

nodes.name: pruneExpiredSessions
nodes.summary: Deletes session files older than the configured retention window from the search dirs based on file mtime.

nodes.name: selectMatchingSession
nodes.summary: Selects the most relevant prior session for the current cwd/project by matching worktree and project header fields in candidate session files.

nodes.name: parseInstinctFile
nodes.summary: Parses an instinct YAML/markdown file into structured instinct records (id, confidence, content) for context injection.

nodes.name: readInstinctsFromDir
nodes.summary: Reads and parses all instinct files (.yml/.yaml/.md) from a directory, tagging each with its scope (project or global).

nodes.name: summarizeActiveInstincts
nodes.summary: Deduplicates instincts across project and global scopes, ranks them by confidence and scope, and renders the top-N actionable instincts as an injectable summary block.

nodes.name: summarizeLearnedSkillFile
nodes.summary: Summarizes a single learned-skill markdown file (heading, when-to-use, first paragraph) into a bounded one-line entry for the session-start context.

nodes.name: summarizeLearnedSkills
nodes.summary: Collects and summarizes the available learned-skill files into a reference-only context block listing the top-N skills.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates SessionStart: prunes expired sessions, writes a session lease, gathers active instincts, prior-session summary (with a historical-reference guard), learned skills, aliases, and project info, then assembles and emits the bounded additionalContext payload.

nodes.name: writeSessionStartPayload
nodes.summary: Serializes the SessionStart hook payload (additionalContext) as JSON and writes it to stdout, flushing reliably before resolving.

nodes.name: formatBatch
nodes.summary: Formats a batch of edited files under one project root using the detected formatter binary, guarding against unsafe path characters when shelling out.

nodes.name: findTsConfigDir
nodes.summary: Walks up from a file path to find the nearest directory containing a tsconfig.json.

nodes.name: typecheckBatch
nodes.summary: Runs tsc --noEmit over a tsconfig dir and filters the output to type errors relevant to the edited files, reporting them to stderr.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Reads and clears the post-edit accumulator, groups edited files by project root (for formatting) and by tsconfig dir (for type-checking), allocates per-batch timeouts, and runs both passes.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: In-process entry point for run-with-flags: invokes main() (catching errors to stderr) and returns the raw stdin pass-through.

nodes.name: cleanupOldCounters
nodes.summary: Removes stale tool-count and context-bucket state files older than the retention window from the temp dir, skipping the current session's files.

nodes.name: incrementToolCallCount
nodes.summary: Atomically reads, increments, and writes the per-session tool-call counter file using a low-level file descriptor with a fallback write.

nodes.name: buildContextSuggestion
nodes.summary: Computes whether a compaction reminder is warranted from the latest transcript usage tokens versus a window-scaled threshold and re-reminder interval, persisting the last context bucket.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the compact suggester: reads the stdin payload, manages per-session counter/bucket state, evaluates the tool-count and context-size signals, and emits any compaction reminder messages.

layers.name: Dispatch & Fan-out
layers.description: Consolidated routers, the run-with-flags wrapper, profile gating, and bootstrap adapters that read stdin and fan each Claude Code lifecycle event out to the individual hook checks.

layers.name: Blocking Gates
layers.description: PreToolUse/Stop hooks that can exit 2 and deny an action — blocking --no-verify bypasses, fact-forcing destructive ops, protecting linter/formatter configs, blocking foreground dev-server commands, and failing low-quality commits.

layers.name: Observation & Telemetry
layers.description: Non-blocking PostToolUse/Stop hooks that aggregate session metrics, track cost and context usage, render the statusline, audit-log commands and PRs/builds, and extract reusable patterns — pure observation that never alters tool results.

layers.name: Quality & Formatting
layers.description: PostToolUse/Stop hooks that lint, format, type-check, and flag console.log or generic-template drift on edited files, warning the agent (with the Stop format/typecheck step able to gate).

layers.name: Session Lifecycle & Context
layers.description: SessionStart/SessionEnd/Stop/PreCompact hooks that inject startup context, persist and clean up session state, manage and suggest compaction, and wire the Cursor session environment.

layers.name: Security
layers.description: Security-focused hooks: the Insaits security monitor with its bridge wrapper, governance-event capture for secrets and policy violations, and the MCP health check.

layers.name: Nudges & Shared Output
layers.description: Non-blocking reminders (git push, tmux), transparent dev-server tmux rewriting, ad-hoc doc-file warnings, desktop notifications, and the shared additionalContext output helper used across the PreToolUse nudges.

tour.title: The Consolidated-Dispatcher Pattern
tour.description: Start at the architectural keystone. Instead of registering dozens of separate hooks in settings.json, ECC registers one thin entry point per Bash lifecycle event (pre-bash-dispatcher.js for PreToolUse, post-bash-dispatcher.js for PostToolUse) that reads stdin and delegates to a single central router, bash-hook-dispatcher.js. That router (the project's highest fan-out node, requiring 12 sibling checks) runs every check in registered order, honors per-hook profile gating, and merges all their results — block decisions, stderr, additionalContext — into one response. This is the single most important lesson for a lean hook layer: fan out internally so Claude Code only ever invokes one hook per event, which keeps config trivial and execution ordering explicit.

tour.title: Enabling and Wrapping a Hook
tour.description: Before any individual check runs, ECC decides whether it should run at all. run-with-flags.js is a generic wrapper that executes a target hook only when enabled by the active profile (ECC_HOOK_PROFILE / ECC_DISABLED_HOOKS), preferring a fast in-process require()+run() and falling back to spawnSync — and it propagates the wrapped hook's exit code, so the same wrapper serves both gates and observers. check-hook-enabled.js is the tiny yes/no CLI that settings.json consults to gate execution, and plugin-hook-bootstrap.js normalizes the plugin root and environment before spawning the real hook. The lesson: a single runtime-gating layer lets you ship many hooks but turn any of them off per profile without editing config.
tour.languageLesson: run-with-flags prefers require()+run() over spawning a child process: loading the hook in-process avoids per-hook Node startup cost (tens of ms each), which matters when one dispatcher fans out to a dozen checks on every Bash call. Hooks opt in by exporting a run(rawInput) function.

tour.title: Blocking Gates — What Exit 2 Looks Like
tour.description: Now meet the hooks that actually deny actions. A blocking gate writes its reason to stderr and exits 2 — Claude Code treats that as a hard refusal. config-protection.js blocks edits to existing linter/formatter configs (so the agent fixes source instead of relaxing checks), gateguard-fact-force.js intercepts the first destructive op of a session and demands grounding facts before allowing a retry, and pre-bash-dev-server-block.js parses the command and refuses foreground dev-server launches that would hang the agent. Study these to see the deliberate shape of a block: detect a narrowly-defined condition, explain why, exit 2 — and nothing else. Everything not matching the condition passes through untouched.
tour.languageLesson: The exit-2 contract: stdout is ignored for blocks, stderr carries the message back to the agent, and exit code 2 is the deny signal. Any other exit code (including 0) lets the action proceed — so a gate that crashes fails OPEN, which is the safe default for a hook layer.

tour.title: The Shared Output Envelope
tour.description: Not every concern warrants a block. The other half of the design is reaching the agent without stopping it. pretooluse-visible-output.js is the project's most-depended-upon helper (fan-in 5): it normalizes and combines additionalContext strings and serializes them into the hookSpecificOutput JSON envelope Claude Code expects, so a hook can surface a warning that the agent sees while the action still proceeds. Centralizing this envelope is why every nudge in ECC speaks with one voice and exits 0. The lesson: separate 'inform' from 'deny' at the plumbing level — a shared output helper for warnings, a bare exit 2 for blocks.

tour.title: Observation & Telemetry — Fail-Open Observers
tour.description: This is the gate's opposite number: hooks that watch and record but never alter the tool result. ecc-metrics-bridge.js maintains a running per-session aggregate (cost, files touched, recent tool hashes) in a temp bridge file so downstream consumers avoid rescanning the transcript JSONL; ecc-context-monitor.js reads that bridge and injects warnings only when thresholds cross (context exhaustion, runaway cost, scope creep, tool loops); cost-tracker.js sums token usage at Stop; and observe-runner.js spawns the continuous-learning observe.sh for the current phase. All of them exit 0 unconditionally — observation must never break the agent. Note the bridge-file pattern: compute once, let many cheap readers share the result.

tour.title: Quality Batching — Defer Expensive Work
tour.description: Formatting and type-checking are too slow to run on every edit, but too valuable to skip. ECC's answer is a producer/consumer pair (a tightly-coupled cluster in the graph): post-edit-accumulator.js fires on each Edit/Write and simply appends the edited JS/TS path to a session-scoped temp file — near-zero latency — while stop-format-typecheck.js fires once at Stop, reads the accumulated list, and batch-formats and type-checks everything grouped by project and tsconfig root. The lesson for a lean hook layer: when work is expensive, accumulate cheaply during the response and pay the cost once per response at the Stop boundary, where a longer timeout is acceptable.
tour.languageLesson: Stop-event hooks get a far more generous timeout (here ~300s) than PreToolUse gates (which should stay under ~200ms with no network). Knowing which lifecycle event you are in tells you how much work you can afford — push anything heavy to Stop.

tour.title: Session Lifecycle & Context Injection
tour.description: Hooks also bracket the whole session. session-start.js injects bounded prior context on startup — it selects the most recent matching session summary, surfaces active instincts and learned skills, prunes expired files, and emits a character-limited additionalContext payload. session-end.js extracts a summary from the transcript and persists a per-session markdown file for cross-session continuity. pre-compact.js preserves state before Claude compacts context, and suggest-compact.js advises manual compaction using a tool-call counter plus the live context-size signal. Together they show how a hook layer can give the agent memory and steer it around the context window — entirely through fail-open observers.

tour.title: Security — Cross-Language Gating
tour.description: The security layer demonstrates how to plug a heavier, possibly cross-language analyzer into the same hook contract. insaits-security-monitor.py runs local anomaly detection on tool input (credential exposure, prompt injection, hallucination chains, 20+ types) and BLOCKS with exit 2 on critical findings while warning via stderr otherwise. Because run-with-flags spawns hooks via node, insaits-security-wrapper.js is a thin JS bridge that forwards stdin to the Python monitor and relays its exit code — including the block. mcp-health-check.js rounds this out by probing MCP server health before tool calls and reconnecting on failure. The lesson: a language-neutral stdin/exit-code contract lets you drop in a Python (or any) analyzer behind a one-line JS shim.

tour.title: Synthesis — Gate vs. Observe
tour.description: Step back and the entire system resolves into one design axis. Blocking gates (block-no-verify.js, which refuses --no-verify commit bypasses via a custom tokenizer) are synchronous, fast, narrowly-scoped, and deliberately fail OPEN — exit 2 only on a confirmed match, exit 0 on anything else including their own errors. Async observers (session-activity-tracker.js, logging sanitized per-tool rows) are non-blocking telemetry that exit 0 unconditionally. Building your own lean hook layer means deciding, for each concern, which side of this axis it lives on, then wiring it behind one consolidated dispatcher and one runtime-gating wrapper. Gate sparingly and precisely; observe freely and never break the agent.
